docs.label: Media Player
docs.description: Primary Home Assistant media_player entity to control, for example media_player.living_room_speaker. Saving a valid entity may reboot the device so the Home Assistant subscription is refreshed.

docs.label: Linked Media Player
docs.description: Optional secondary media_player entity used when the main speaker switches to a TV or Line-in source. Leave empty to disable. Saving a valid entity may reboot the device so the Home Assistant subscription is refreshed.

docs.label: Day/Night Source
docs.description: Optional binary_sensor or input_boolean entity to control day/night mode. On means day and off means night. Leave empty to use sun.sun.

docs.label: Track Clock
docs.description: On shows time remaining; off shows the track length. Tap the time label on the device to toggle this at any time.

docs.label: Show Progress Bar
docs.description: Shows or hides the playback progress bar.

docs.label: Auto-Show Track Info
docs.description: Shows track title and artist automatically when new playback information arrives. Turn this off to keep square screens focused on artwork and the progress bar; tap the screen to show or hide the info manually. Shown on the ESP32-S3 4848S040 and ESP32-P4 86 Panel.

docs.label: Track Info Duration
docs.description: How long track info stays visible after artwork loads or playback starts. Choose Always to keep track info visible and never auto-hide it. Shown on the ESP32-S3 4848S040 and ESP32-P4 86 Panel.

docs.label: Speaker Panel Auto-Close
docs.description: Turns automatic closing of the volume/speaker panel on or off.

docs.label: Timer
docs.description: Time without touch interaction before the speaker panel automatically closes and returns to the now-playing view.

docs.label: Day Active Brightness
docs.description: Screen brightness during active use in daytime mode.

docs.label: Night Active Brightness
docs.description: Screen brightness during active use in night mode.

docs.label: Day Dimmed Brightness
docs.description: Brightness after the paused dimming timer during the day.

docs.label: Night Dimmed Brightness
docs.description: Brightness after the paused dimming timer at night.

docs.label: Dim when idle
docs.description: Turns paused idle dimming on or off.

docs.label: Dim After
docs.description: Time after playback pauses before the screen dims.

docs.label: Screen Saver
docs.description: Turns the clock/off screen saver step on or off. When off, the browser hides the screen saver options.

docs.label: Start Screen Saver After
docs.description: Time after dimming before the idle action runs.

docs.label: Day Clock Brightness
docs.description: Backlight level for the daytime clock screen saver. Shown when the daytime action is Clock.

docs.label: Evening Clock Brightness
docs.description: Backlight level for the evening clock screen saver. Shown when the evening action is Clock.

docs.label: Daytime Screen Saver
docs.description: What happens during the day: Clock, Screen Off, or Disabled.

docs.label: Evening Screen Saver
docs.description: What happens in the evening: Clock, Screen Off, or Disabled.

docs.label: Schedule Screen Off
docs.description: Turns the daily scheduled screen-off behavior on or off.

docs.label: On Time
docs.description: Hour when the screen becomes available again.

docs.label: Off Time
docs.description: Hour when the screen turns off for the schedule.

docs.label: When Woken, Idle Time To Screen Off
docs.description: How long a temporary touch wake lasts during scheduled off hours.

docs.label: Day Screen Warmth
docs.description: Warmth applied to album art during the day. 0% means no tint, 100% means maximum warmth.

docs.label: Night Screen Warmth
docs.description: Warmth applied to album art at night. 0% means no tint, 100% means maximum warmth.

docs.label: Clock Format
docs.description: Display the clock screen saver in 24-hour or 12-hour format. The 12-hour format omits AM/PM so the large clock still fits and can drift for burn-in prevention.

docs.label: Timezone
docs.description: IANA-style timezone for the clock and scheduled screen-off controls. The browser shows the current GMT offset.

docs.label: Screen Rotation
docs.description: Rotation control shown only on devices that support changing orientation from the browser UI.

docs.label: Auto Update
docs.description: When enabled, firmware updates are installed automatically when detected.

docs.label: Update Frequency
docs.description: How often the device checks for updates. Hidden when Auto Update is off.

docs.label: Install Update
docs.description: Installs the checked update when one is available.

docs.label: Check for Update
docs.description: Checks public releases without installing. The same browser button changes to Install Update after a newer version is found.

docs_sections.title: Volume
docs_sections.summary: The volume section controls whether the speaker panel closes automatically after a short delay.

docs_sections.title: Screen Saver
docs_sections.summary: The browser Settings tab groups screen saver controls into idle screen dimming, screen saver timing, and night schedule behavior. See Screen Saver for the full behavior details.

docs_sections.title: Device
docs_sections.summary: The browser Device tab contains clock settings, day/night source, active screen brightness, screen tone, supported rotation controls, and firmware update controls.

docs_sections.title: Screen Tone
docs_sections.summary: Shifts album art toward warmer colors for a more comfortable viewing experience, especially at night. Separate day and night sliders follow the same day/night detection used by screen brightness. The tint updates instantly without reloading the image.
